name: Cash America Pawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international pawn operator with 3,300+ locations across the U.S., Latin America, and UK, offering pawn loans secured by personal property and retail merchandise sales.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc., headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas, is the dominant player in the global pawn industry. Founded and operating as a publicly traded company (component of S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000 indices), FirstCash has built the largest international pawn network spanning 29 U.S. states, Washington D.C., the United Kingdom, and Latin America (Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, El Salvador). The company employs approximately 22,000 people across these regions.

FirstCash's core business is providing pawn loans—non-recourse loans secured by pledged personal property. Customers can use jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments, and other valuable items as collateral to receive immediate cash. Beyond lending, FirstCash operates a substantial retail merchandise business, buying and selling items from their inventory. The company also offers ancillary services including layaway plans (10% down payment required), gold/precious metals buying, and retail sales of secondhand goods. Through its AFF subsidiary, FirstCash also provides lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions via 15,000+ merchant partner locations nationwide.

FirstCash differentiates itself through scale, geographic reach, and operational sophistication. With over 3,300 retail locations, the company offers unmatched accessibility for consumers seeking quick cash without credit checks. Their inventory diversity—spanning jewelry to musical instruments—provides both lending collateral and retail merchandise options. The company's public market standing and institutional infrastructure suggest professional compliance and consumer protection standards typical of large financial operators.

Honestly assessed, FirstCash serves cash-constrained consumers effectively but operates within the inherent limitations of pawn lending: customers must surrender valuable property, loans are typically small and short-term, and interest rates/fees on pawn loans, while legal, can be substantial. The company is most suitable for individuals needing immediate cash who have non-essential valuables and no access to traditional credit. FirstCash's scale makes it reliable and transparent, but pawn lending remains fundamentally transactional rather than wealth-building.
pros: 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, D.C., UK, and Latin America provide exceptional accessibility
No credit check required—serves credit-constrained and unbanked consumers effectively
Immediate cash on-the-spot transactions with no application delays
Publicly traded company (S&P MidCap 400, Russell 2000) provides institutional oversight and transparency
Non-recourse loans mean no personal liability if collateral is forfeited
Diverse collateral acceptance (jewelry, electronics, tools, instruments, appliances, sporting goods)
Layaway service option offers alternative 10% down payment plan for purchases
cons: Pawn loans are short-term and typically small, not suitable for major financial needs or consolidation
Requires surrendering valuable personal property as collateral, creating personal loss risk if unable to repay
Interest rates and fees on pawn loans, while legal, can be significantly higher than traditional loans or payday alternatives
Not a credit-building service—pawn loans do not report to credit bureaus, so they cannot improve credit scores
Retail merchandise quality and selection vary by location; inventory not guaranteed
best_for: Unbanked or credit-constrained consumers needing immediate cash without credit checks
Individuals with valuable non-essential items (jewelry, electronics, instruments) willing to use as collateral
People seeking short-term bridge loans between paychecks with valuable property to pledge
Consumers interested in browsing affordable secondhand merchandise or layaway purchases
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for unbanked or no-credit consumers needing immediate cash who own valuable non-essential items and can access one of their 3,300+ locations. The primary caveat is that pawn loans are short-term, secured transactions requiring collateral surrender—not suitable for long-term financing, credit building, or consumers without valuable assets to pledge.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, instruments)
Retail sales of secondhand merchandise from pawn inventory
Gold, silver, and precious metals buying—customers receive cash on-the-spot
Layaway plans with 10% down payment option
Item purchasing—FirstCash buys almost anything of value
Store locator service to find nearest pawn location
Lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions (via AFF subsidiary subsidiary with 15,000+ merchant partners)
